turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some pretty uncomfortable symptoms for the past three days. It started with a tightness in my chest that feels like pressure, and I'm worried about what might be causing it.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: The tightness in my chest is pretty consistent - it's like someone has their hand on my sternum, pressing down. It also makes it hard to breathe when I'm exerting myself.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: I've been noticing some sweating during the episodes of chest tightness, and sometimes I feel a little queasy. But like I said, it's mainly just that pressure feeling in my chest.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Yes, definitely some sweating - it's pretty noticeable during those episodes of chest tightness.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: No perspiration at all during the episodes.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The pressure feeling is mainly just in my sternum area. Occasionally, I'll feel a bit of discomfort when the episode gets really bad, but it's not consistent.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: I've noticed that during some of the more severe episodes, I feel a tingling sensation in my left arm. It's hard to describe, but it's like pins and needles.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: Only with specific questioning about radiation of pain or numbness/tingling in the arms. I've already mentioned that during some more severe episodes, I feel a tingling sensation in my left arm, but I'm not sure if it's related to the chest tightness or something else.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: Just mild bilateral ankle swelling lately, nothing too out of the ordinary.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: Only mild bilateral ankle swelling, to be honest. My shoes feel a bit tighter than usual because of it.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: Yeah, my shoes have been feeling a bit tighter than usual due to some ankle swelling I've noticed lately. It's nothing too severe, but it's definitely noticeable when I'm putting on my boots for work.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Actually, yes - I do experience some mild nausea during these episodes of chest tightness. It's not severe, but it's definitely there.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: No, I don't feel sick to my stomach during those episodes.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: No, I haven't had any of those symptoms. Just the chest tightness and shortness of breath when I exert myself.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: No, my appetite and weight have been pretty normal lately.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: I've been eating a lot of fast food lately - 5-6 days a week. To be honest, my job as a truck driver is pretty sedentary and I don't make time for exercise.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: I'm not very active at all. As a truck driver, I spend most of my days sitting in the cab, and when I'm home, I don't really have time to exercise.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: I know my diet isn't ideal - I tend to grab fast food or convenience store snacks when I'm in a rush, which can be most days.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My father had a heart attack at 52 and unfortunately passed away. My mom has hypertension and type 2 diabetes, but she's still going strong at 78.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: I try to remember to take my medication when I can, but sometimes it slips my mind due to my irregular schedule.
